armies.name: Vigilant Brotherhood
armies.tagline: Ruination-chamber elite from Skaventide / Ultimate Starter Set
armies.playstyle: Prosecutors flank at speed while Liberators hold objectives. Holy Orders offers a run-and-charge trick and a once-per-battle Ward (5+).

battleTraits.name: Storm Charge
battleTraits.summary: Pick a unit not in combat; it may use Charge abilities this turn even if it used a Run ability.
battleTraits.timing: Once per battle, Your Charge Phase

battleTraits.name: Shield of Azyr
battleTraits.summary: Pick a friendly unit; it has Ward (5+) until the start of your next turn.
battleTraits.timing: Once per battle, Your Hero Phase

regimentAbilities.name: Strike Where Needed
regimentAbilities.summary: When a unit uses Retreat, it takes no mortal damage and may still use Charge abilities this turn.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Good when you want mobile Prosecutors or hit-and-run Liberators.
regimentAbilities.timing: Once per battle, reaction to Retreat

regimentAbilities.name: Blaze of Glory
regimentAbilities.summary: Pick a unit in combat; when its models are slain, roll D6 per slain model — on 4+ inflict 1 mortal on an enemy in combat with that unit.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Good when you expect your infantry to trade on an objective.
regimentAbilities.timing: Once per battle, Any Combat Phase

enhancements.name: Hallowed Scrolls
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Safest pick — keeps your Lord-Vigilant alive longer.

enhancements.name: Morrda's Talon
enhancements.summary: Your general's Hallowed Greataxe has Crit (Mortal).
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Aggressive pick if you plan to seek combat with your general.

enhancements.name: Quicksilver Draught
enhancements.summary: Your general has Strike-first this phase.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Save for a decisive combat phase when you need to strike first.
enhancements.timing: Once per battle, Any Combat Phase

enhancements.name: Null Pendant
enhancements.summary: Roll for each enemy contesting the same objective as your general; on 2+ subtract the roll from their control score this turn.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Objective play — use when contesting a key point late game.
enhancements.timing: Once per battle, End of Any Turn

armies.name: Yndrasta's Spearhead
armies.tagline: Lightning-strike force from the original AoS starter set
armies.playstyle: Yndrasta and Annihilators arrive from round 3. Vanquishers and a Stormstrike Chariot hit first; Yndrasta finishes the biggest threats.

battleTraits.name: Lightning-Strike Arrival
battleTraits.summary: Yndrasta and Annihilators are not deployed. From battle round 3, set them up anywhere more than 6" from all enemy units.

regimentAbilities.name: Drive Them Back
regimentAbilities.summary: Pick units contesting an objective and in combat; each can pile-in, then roll D6 per adjacent enemy — on 4+ inflict 1 mortal.
regimentAbilities.timing: Once per battle, Your Movement Phase

regimentAbilities.name: Defend to the Last
regimentAbilities.summary: Friendly units have Ward (6+) while contesting an objective you control.

enhancements.name: The Prime Huntress
enhancements.summary: Thengavar has Damage 2D6 against Monster targets.

enhancements.name: Dazzling Radiance
enhancements.summary: If your general was set up this phase, return 1 slain model to each friendly unit wholly within 12" of them.
enhancements.timing: Once per battle, Your Movement Phase

enhancements.name: Strike with the Tempest's Rage
enhancements.summary: Your general has Strike-first if they charged this turn.

enhancements.name: Hawk of the Celestial Skies
enhancements.summary: Add 1 to hit rolls for friendly units wholly within 12" of your general this phase.
enhancements.timing: Once per battle, End of Any Turn

armies.name: Gnawfeast Clawpack
armies.tagline: Swarm melee from Skaventide / Ultimate Starter Set
armies.playstyle: Tunnel reserves and Gnawhole Ambush threaten the back line. Clanrats swarm objectives while Rat Ogors and heroes deliver the killing blow.

battleTraits.name: The Lurking Vermintide
battleTraits.summary: Pick an undeployed unit; it starts in the tunnels below. Units still there after round 3 without using Gnawhole Ambush are destroyed.

battleTraits.name: Gnawhole Ambush
battleTraits.summary: Pick a unit in the tunnels below; set it up within 6" of a battlefield corner and more than 9" from all enemy units.

regimentAbilities.name: Warpstone-Laced Bullets
regimentAbilities.summary: Pick a friendly unit's ranged weapon; it has Crit (Mortal) this phase.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Best with Warlock Engineer or ranged attacks before a charge.
regimentAbilities.timing: Once per battle, Your Shooting Phase

regimentAbilities.name: Too Quick to Hit-Hit
regimentAbilities.summary: No mortal damage is inflicted on friendly units when they use Retreat abilities.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Reliable pick — Clanrats can retreat from trouble without losing models.

enhancements.name: Lead the Seething Horde
enhancements.summary: When using Call for Reinforcements, the replacement unit can be set up wholly within 13" of your general and not in combat.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Pairs well with Clanrats — bring reinforcements closer to the fight.

enhancements.name: Warpstone Charm
enhancements.summary: Subtract 1 from save rolls for enemy units in combat with your general.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Aggressive — your Clawlord wants to be in melee.

enhancements.name: Skryre Connections
enhancements.summary: Your general's Ratling Pistol has Attacks 2D6 instead of D6.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: More shots from the Clawlord's pistol — risky but fun.

armies.name: Warpspark Clawpack
armies.playstyle: Clanrats screen for Stormfiends and a Warp Lightning Cannon. Always Three Clawsteps Ahead lets you reposition in the enemy movement phase.

battleTraits.name: Always Three Clawsteps Ahead
battleTraits.summary: Pick a friendly unit not in combat; it can use Normal Move as if it were your movement phase.
battleTraits.timing: Once per phase, Enemy Movement Phase

regimentAbilities.name: Warpstone-Laced Armour
regimentAbilities.summary: When an attack targets your Stormfiends, they have Ward (5+) for the rest of the turn.
regimentAbilities.timing: Once per battle, reaction to attack on Stormfiends

regimentAbilities.name: Endless Swarm of Rats
regimentAbilities.summary: When Clanrats use Seething Swarm, return D6 slain models instead of D3.

enhancements.name: Skilled Manipulator
enhancements.summary: Your general has Ward (4+) while within 1" of any friendly Clanrats units.

enhancements.name: Cage of Warp Lightning
enhancements.summary: Pick an enemy within 6" and roll: on 2+ they have Strike-last; on 1 inflict 1 mortal on your general.
enhancements.timing: Once per battle, Any Combat Phase

enhancements.name: Skitterleap
enhancements.summary: Casting roll 2D6; on 6+ remove your general and set up more than 6" from enemies. Cannot move next movement phase.

enhancements.name: Scurry Away
enhancements.summary: Roll a dice; on 3+ your general may Retreat immediately with no mortal damage.

armies.name: Crixxit's Kill-Pack
armies.playstyle: Glass cannon with teleporting heroes and reinforced infantry. Fragile but explosive.

armies.name: Castelite Company
armies.playstyle: Sturdy infantry lines with solid saves. Dependable but slower to close games.

armies.name: Fusil Platoon
armies.playstyle: Strong shooting with limited frontline durability. Controls space from range.

armies.name: Sentinels of Embergard
armies.playstyle: Positioning-heavy synergies. Gallants hit hard with setup but the army is fragile.

armies.name: Heartflayer Troupe
armies.playstyle: Strike first, hit hard, die fast. Aggressive and unforgiving.

armies.name: Khainite Shadow Coven
armies.playstyle: Many units, movement tricks, and defensive manipulation. High skill ceiling.

armies.name: Saga Axeband
armies.playstyle: Headlong charges and Awaken the Runes. Surprisingly durable despite low saves.

armies.name: Skyhammer Task Force
armies.playstyle: Positioning and precision fire. Controls the board from range.

armies.name: Grundstok Trailblazers
armies.playstyle: Flying 3+ saves, Endrinrigger reinforcements, and a dominant general.

armies.name: Soulraid Hunt
armies.playstyle: Excellent mobility and burst damage. One of the most dangerous Spearheads.

armies.name: Akhelian Tide Guard
armies.playstyle: Fast with double eel units. King buffs saves; Reavers provide ranged pressure.

armies.name: Glittering Phalanx
armies.playstyle: Hits hard but fragile. Rewards precise play; getting charged hurts.

armies.name: Hurakan Vanguard
armies.playstyle: Windchargers and fox aura frustrate enemy hammer units. Strong board control.

armies.name: Starscale Warhost
armies.playstyle: Tough Saurus Warriors and a devastating Carnosaur charge.

armies.name: Sunblooded Prowlers
armies.playstyle: Hard-hitting and fast but extremely fragile. High skill cap.

armies.name: Bitterbark Copse
armies.playstyle: Tough units with staying power. Clever positioning is key.

armies.name: Bloodbound Gore Pilgrims
armies.playstyle: Excellent saves and brutal melee. Dominates most matchups.

armies.name: Fangs of the Blood God
armies.playstyle: Reinforced keyword for hitting power. Must finish fights quickly.

armies.name: Fluxblade Coven
armies.playstyle: Unpredictable spell use and positioning. Fragile but potent in skilled hands.

armies.name: Blades of the Lurid Dream
armies.playstyle: Races across the board and hits hard but cannot take punishment.

armies.name: Bleak Host
armies.playstyle: Shrugs off chip damage with surprising mobility for Nurgle.

armies.name: Bloodwind Legion
armies.playstyle: Strong leadership buffs. Hits hard and holds objectives well.

armies.name: Darkoath Raiders
armies.playstyle: Early objective grabs and melee pressure. Light armour limits prolonged fights.

armies.name: Helforge Host
armies.playstyle: Daemonic Power buffs, 2+ saves, and a bull-centaur monster. Grinds opponents down.

armies.name: Bad Moon Madmob
armies.playstyle: Great board presence and objective play. Random and swingy.

armies.name: Snarlpack Huntaz
armies.playstyle: Movement 12" and retreat-and-charge tricks. Can lock the board if going first.

armies.name: Ironjawz Bigmob
armies.playstyle: Mass 3+ saves and -1 rend. Get stuck in and smash face.

armies.name: Swampskulka Gang
armies.playstyle: Poisoned weapons and sneaky tricks. Different feel from Ironjawz.

armies.name: Tyrant's Bellow
armies.playstyle: Good saves, brutal damage, surprising mobility. Dominates by turn 3–4.

armies.name: Scrapglutt
armies.playstyle: Consistent damage without Tyrant's Bellow's explosive peak.

armies.name: Wallsmasher Stomp
armies.playstyle: Few models but massive impact. Simple and devastating.

armies.name: Carrion Retainers
armies.playstyle: Delusion mechanics and 6+ ward. Sneaky objective grabs.

armies.name: Charnel Watch
armies.playstyle: Random delusions and hard-hitting charges. Fragile but fun.

armies.name: Slasher Host
armies.playstyle: Charge potential and objective harassment. Lacks staying power.

armies.name: Cursed Shacklehorde
armies.playstyle: Many units, Fly everywhere, 5+ ward. Hit-and-fade with resilience.

armies.name: Tithe-Reaper Echelon
armies.playstyle: Synergy between units. Grinds through sheer resilience.

armies.name: Mortisan Elite
armies.playstyle: Powerful support pieces. Fragile if key heroes are removed.

armies.name: Kavalos Vanguard
armies.playstyle: Free retreats, Fly, and charge-blocking. Wins through movement control.

armies.name: Bloodcrave Hunt
armies.playstyle: Excellent mobility, recursion, and objective control.

armies.name: Deathrattle Tomb Host
armies.playstyle: Defensive with reinforcements. Wight King must stay near troops for 5+ ward.

matchSteps.title: Choose Spearhead Armies
matchSteps.summary: Each player picks their faction and specific Spearhead army.
matchSteps.body: Spearhead armies are fixed box-set rosters — you cannot change unit composition. Many factions have multiple Spearhead options with different playstyles. Pick the army each player is fielding today.
tips: Check which Spearhead box your models came from if you're unsure.
Download free faction Spearhead packs from Warhammer Community for warscrolls.

matchSteps.title: Roll for Attacker & Defender
matchSteps.body: Roll off to determine who picks attacker and defender. The attacker picks regiment abilities and enhancements first, then the defender. The defender also chooses which realm side of the battlefield to fight on.
tips: Going first in round 1 lets the attacker choose who takes the first turn.
Consider your army's strengths when choosing attacker vs defender.

matchSteps.title: Pick Regiment Abilities
matchSteps.body: Each Spearhead army offers two regiment abilities. The attacker picks one, then the defender picks one. These range from passive bonuses to once-per-battle actions that can shift the game.
tips: Some abilities counter specific opponents — study the enemy army before choosing.
Passive abilities are reliable; once-per-battle abilities can be game-swinging.

matchSteps.title: Pick General Enhancements
matchSteps.body: Each army has four enhancements for its general. The attacker picks one, then the defender. Enhancements upgrade your general only — protect them accordingly.
tips: Ward enhancements help fragile generals survive longer.
Offensive enhancements can turn your general into a linchpin — but losing them hurts.

matchSteps.title: Set Up the Battlefield
matchSteps.summary: Defender chooses board and side; terrain, objectives, deploy armies.
matchSteps.body:
Deployment is the last step before round 1. The defender chooses which Spearhead battlefield you are using — Fire and Jade (Aqshy or Ghyran), Sand and Bone (Ossia or Dolorum), or City of Ash (Ashen Bastion or Shattered Crossroads) — and which side of that board to fight on. Each side has its own deployment map and twist deck, so match the deck to the side you pick.

Next, set up terrain: two large and two small features, spread across the board without blocking deployment zones or objectives. Objectives are already printed on the board — the whole circle counts when contesting, not just the centre symbol.

Deploy armies within the shaded zones on the deployment map, defender first then alternating. Before the first turn, resolve any once-per-battle deployment abilities on your army sheets (for example, Skaven hiding a unit in the tunnels below).
tips: Use the coin flip in the checklist below if neither player cares which side you play on.
Fire and Jade is the classic Spearhead board; Sand and Bone and City of Ash add new maps and twists.
Check both army sheets for Deployment Phase abilities — they are easy to forget in a first game.
The whole objective circle counts, not just the centre symbol.

matchSteps.title: Fight the Battle
matchSteps.summary: Play 4 battle rounds — score victory points to win.
matchSteps.body: Deploy armies, then play exactly four battle rounds (~60–90 minutes). Each round: priority roll, identify underdog, draw twist card, draw battle tactic cards, then alternate turns. Score VP for objectives and completed battle tactics.
tips: Twist cards favour the underdog — comebacks are built in.
Each battle tactic card offers a tactic OR a command — not both.
